# 3. Scenario

We are interested in exploring the implications of allowing AI, a service generated by the information industry, to replace labor at various costs, for different industries and for different regions. Specifically, we focus on clerical labor which is likely to be most easily replaced by AI in all industries.

We implement this scenario with several modalities. First, we consider two degrees of global adoption of the AI labor replacement technology: (1) an adoption by the developed countries only and (2) a global adoption. The second modality relates to the supply of clerical labor during the proces of adopting AI to replace it; we assume that (1) the supply of clerical labor remains fixed and that the wage will adjust, and, alternatively that (2) the wage remains fixed and, instead, clerical labor will exit/enter the labor force to maintian this wage.

```{=html}
<!-- ## Replacing professional staff with AI

In the second set of scenarios, we consider the replacement of technical staff with AI in the manufacturing and service sector. Again, we implement the adotpion of AI in three stages, first by each country alon, then by all developed countries and then, finally, by the entire world. We, again, test two different costs for AI implementation as in the scenarios mentioned above.

## Putting it all together

In the final set of scenarios we analyze the situation where both clerical labor in all industries and technical staff in manufacturing and services is replaced by AI at different costs by developed countries and the entire world.



 -->
```
